skill.name: dripcampaignskill
skill.description: Plan, review, and improve email drip campaigns with clear sequence logic, scoring hooks, approval gates, and conversion checks.
skill.summary: Build safer nurture sequences with branching logic, measurement plans, and human review checkpoints.
skill.author: Sequenzy

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives caused by ordinary words such as description, design, desired, and destructive. The system reconnaissance finding at SKILL.md line 27 is also a false positive; no command execution, data collection, prompt injection, or exfiltration behavior was found in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged text at these locations as weak cryptography, but the content is ordinary campaign guidance and checklist language. No cryptographic algorithm, hash function, or security-sensitive implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain plain English email campaign instructions. There is no code, encryption call, hashing call, or configuration that could implement weak cryptography.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 27, but that line is a section heading for cases where the skill should not be used. No host inspection, network probing, environment discovery, or reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line is markdown documentation, not executable behavior. The surrounding file describes approval boundaries for email work and contains no reconnaissance instructions.

content.value_statement: Email nurture programs often fail because timing, branching, scoring, and approvals are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured plans, audits, and review-ready recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Maps drip campaign entry points, sequence steps, delays, branches, and exits.
Reviews lead scoring hooks and handoff thresholds for sales or onboarding teams.
Diagnoses drop-off patterns by message, branch, source, segment, and call to action.
Creates nurture briefs, branch logic tables, and conversion-focused rewrite plans.
Separates recommendations from live email system actions that need approval.
Defines measurement checks for timing, audience fit, offers, and copy changes.
limitations: Does not send emails, import contacts, or edit production automations by itself.
Requires accurate campaign data, audience context, and platform constraints from the user.
Cannot confirm consent, suppression, or legal compliance without source material.
Provides operational guidance, not provider-specific account access or execution.

use_cases.title: Plan a nurture sequence
use_cases.description: Create a staged drip map with clear message jobs, timing, branches, and exit criteria.

use_cases.title: Review automation logic
use_cases.description: Check whether branches, scoring hooks, and handoffs are measurable and ready for team review.

use_cases.title: Diagnose campaign drop-off
use_cases.description: Separate timing, segment, offer, and copy problems before changing the full sequence.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a basic drip map
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to draft a drip campaign for [audience] after [entry event]. Include the sequence goal, email jobs, delays, exits, and approval checks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an existing sequence
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit this drip campaign: [paste sequence details]. Identify weak branches, unclear scoring hooks, missing exits, and metrics needed before changes.

prompt_templates.title: Improve conversion flow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to diagnose drop-off in [campaign name]. Compare message performance by source and segment, then recommend sequence logic changes before copy rewrites.

prompt_templates.title: Design a handoff model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design lead scoring and sales handoff rules for [campaign]. Include triggers, thresholds, ownership, approval gates, and recovery paths.

output_examples.input: Plan a post-webinar nurture sequence for finance leads who attended but did not request a demo.
output_examples.output: A staged nurture brief with the entry moment, audience intent, four email jobs, branch criteria for engagement, sales handoff thresholds, exits, and review checkpoints.

output_examples.input: Audit this onboarding drip because many users stop after email three.
output_examples.output: A drop-off diagnosis that separates timing, audience fit, product proof, and call-to-action issues, then lists prioritized fixes for review.

output_examples.input: Create scoring hooks for a trial education sequence.
output_examples.output: A scoring plan with observable behaviors, qualification thresholds, owner actions, suppression checks, and metrics for success or recovery.

best_practices: Provide audience, entry source, current sequence steps, delays, branches, and conversion goals.
Share message-level metrics by source and segment before asking for performance conclusions.
Request explicit approval before live sends, imports, suppression changes, or production automation edits.
anti_patterns: Using the skill for generic email advice without lifecycle, platform, evidence, or approval context.
Asking it to assume consent, suppression status, or missing contact fields.
Skipping sequence logic review and rewriting every email first.

faq.question: Can this skill send emails?
faq.answer: No. It creates plans, reviews, and recommendations. Live sends require explicit human approval and platform access.

faq.question: What information should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the audience, entry event, current steps, delays, branches, metrics, offers, and desired conversion.

faq.question: Does it support lead scoring?
faq.answer: Yes. It can propose scoring hooks and handoff thresholds when teams can act on those signals.

faq.question: Can it diagnose low conversion?
faq.answer: Yes. It separates timing, audience fit, offer, call-to-action, and copy issues before recommending changes.

faq.question: Does it replace compliance review?
faq.answer: No. It does not verify consent, suppression rules, legal requirements, or account-specific policy.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
